evidence_quote: Stafford trains the inexperienced recruits near the village of Oxcross, within three days ride from Lannisport.
asserted_relation: Battle of Oxcross took place at the village of Oxcross

evidence_quote: The northmen crept into my uncle's camp and cut his horse lines, and Lord Stark sent his wolf among them. Even war-trained destriers went mad. Knights were trampled to death in their pavilions, and the rabble woke in terror and fled, casting aside their weapons to run the faster.
asserted_relation: Robb Stark commanded the night attack at Oxcross, directing the horse-line cutting and releasing Grey Wind

evidence_quote: Ser Stafford was slain as he chased after a horse. Lord Rickard Karstark drove a lance through his chest.
asserted_relation: Stafford Lannister was killed at the Battle of Oxcross

evidence_quote: Ser Stafford was slain as he chased after a horse. Lord Rickard Karstark drove a lance through his chest.
asserted_relation: Rickard Karstark killed Stafford Lannister at the Battle of Oxcross

evidence_quote: Your brother had his direwolf with him, but I suspect that's as far as it went. The northmen crept into my uncle's camp and cut his horse lines, and Lord Stark sent his wolf among them. Even war-trained destriers went mad. Knights were trampled to death in their pavilions, and the rabble woke in terror and fled, casting aside their weapons to run the faster.
asserted_relation: Grey Wind was loosed among the Lannister horses causing the stampede that decided the battle

evidence_quote: old Ser Stevron died at Oxcross, we hear.
asserted_relation: Stevron Frey was wounded at Oxcross and died shortly after

evidence_quote: Half a hundred more have been taken captive, including Jast's sons and my nephew Martyn Lannister.
asserted_relation: Martyn Lannister was captured as a prisoner at the Battle of Oxcross

evidence_quote: Having had their outriders and scouts eliminated, the Lannister force is unaware of their commander's defeat or of Robb's army's approach, and is still divided in three camps in order to besiege Riverrun properly.
asserted_relation: The Battle of the Camps was fought in the three Lannister siege camps surrounding Riverrun

evidence_quote: It was Robb … and Brynden. Your brother is here as well, my lord.
asserted_relation: Robb Stark commanded the attack at the Battle of the Camps, leading two columns of armored horse to overrun the middle Lannister camp

evidence_quote: It was Robb … and Brynden. Your brother is here as well, my lord.
asserted_relation: Brynden Tully (the Blackfish) led Robb's van and attacked the north Lannister camp at the Battle of the Camps

evidence_quote: Lord Jon Umber burns the siege towers which were under construction, and Lord Blackwood releases the prisoners taken in the earlier battle under the walls of Riverrun, including Edmure.
asserted_relation: Jon Umber (the Greatjon) burned the Lannister siege towers during the Battle of the Camps

evidence_quote: It had been Lord Tytos who led the sortie that plucked her brother from the Lannister camp.
asserted_relation: Tytos Blackwood led the sortie from Riverrun that freed Edmure Tully and broke the Lannister middle camp from the rear

evidence_quote: It had been Lord Tytos who led the sortie that plucked her brother from the Lannister camp.
asserted_relation: Edmure Tully was held prisoner in the Lannister camp at Riverrun and was freed by Tytos Blackwood's sortie during the battle

evidence_quote: Lord Andros Brax leads his men from the middle camp to the rafts and attempts to make a crossing. Riverrun begins pelting the rafts with thrown rocks, however, and soon the rafts are flipping and sinking. Among those drowned is Lord Brax.
asserted_relation: Andros Brax drowned attempting to cross the river to reinforce the north camp during the battle

evidence_quote: Forley leads the retreat of two thousand spears and two thousand bowmen when he sees the battle is lost.
asserted_relation: Forley Prester commanded the southern Lannister camp and led the retreat when the battle was lost

evidence_quote: Having had their outriders and scouts eliminated, the Lannister force is unaware of their commander's defeat or of Robb's army's approach, and is still divided in three camps in order to besiege Riverrun properly.
asserted_relation: Jaime Lannister commanded the three-camp siege of Riverrun that became the Battle of the Camps; he was already captured at Whispering Wood before the battle itself and absent during it

evidence_quote: Grey Wind, Robb's direwolf, allegedly kills four men and a dozen horses.
asserted_relation: Grey Wind killed men and horses during the Battle of the Camps, fighting alongside Robb Stark

evidence_quote: I saw. Last night, when it began, I told them … had to see. They carried me to the gatehouse … watched from the battlements. Ah, that was beautiful … the torches came in a wave, I could hear the cries floating across the river … sweet cries … when that siege tower went up, gods … would have died then, and glad, if only I could have seen you children first. Was it your boy who did it? Was it your Robb?
asserted_relation: Hoster Tully watched the Battle of the Camps from the battlements of Riverrun, ailing and bedridden but carried to the gatehouse to witness

evidence_quote: Below, beneath the stone-and-timber battlements of a small castle, a melee was in progress.
asserted_relation: The melee took place at Bitterbridge, beneath the castle

evidence_quote: She heard King Renly declare the Lady Brienne of Tarth the victor of the great melee at Bitterbridge, last mounted of one hundred sixteen knights.
asserted_relation: Brienne of Tarth won the melee at Bitterbridge, last of 116 knights

evidence_quote: Loras Tyrell, on the bottom, took the brunt of the impact. The blue knight pulled a long dirk free and flicked open Tyrell's visor. The roar of the crowd was too loud for Catelyn to hear what Ser Loras said, but she saw the word form on his split, bloody lips. Yield.
asserted_relation: Loras Tyrell competed and was the last fighter defeated by Brienne, yielding to her

evidence_quote: Brienne of Tarth defeats Ser Ronnet Connington
asserted_relation: Ronnet Connington competed in the melee and was defeated by Brienne

evidence_quote: She heard King Renly declare the Lady Brienne of Tarth the victor of the great melee at Bitterbridge, last mounted of one hundred sixteen knights. "As champion, you may ask of me any boon that you desire. If it lies in my power, it is yours."
asserted_relation: Renly Baratheon presided as king, hosted the melee, and declared Brienne victor

evidence_quote: Below, beneath the stone-and-timber battlements of a small castle, a melee was in progress.
asserted_relation: Catelyn Stark was present as Robb's envoy and witnessed the melee

evidence_quote: Lord Lorent Caswell (host)
asserted_relation: Lorent Caswell was the host at whose castle the melee was held

evidence_quote: She recognized Lord Mathis Rowan, stouter and more florid than ever, the golden tree of his House spread across his white doublet. Below him sat Lady Oakheart, tiny and delicate, and to her left Lord Randyll Tarly of Horn Hill, his greatsword Heartsbane propped up against the back of his seat.
asserted_relation: Lady Arwyn Oakheart attended the melee as a spectator in the gallery

evidence_quote: She recognized Lord Mathis Rowan, stouter and more florid than ever, the golden tree of his House spread across his white doublet. Below him sat Lady Oakheart, tiny and delicate, and to her left Lord Randyll Tarly of Horn Hill, his greatsword Heartsbane propped up against the back of his seat.
asserted_relation: Lord Mathis Rowan attended the melee as a spectator in the gallery

evidence_quote: She recognized Lord Mathis Rowan, stouter and more florid than ever, the golden tree of his House spread across his white doublet. Below him sat Lady Oakheart, tiny and delicate, and to her left Lord Randyll Tarly of Horn Hill, his greatsword Heartsbane propped up against the back of his seat.
asserted_relation: Lord Randyll Tarly attended the melee as a spectator in the gallery

evidence_quote: Queen Margaery Tyrell
asserted_relation: Queen Margaery Tyrell attended the melee as a spectator

evidence_quote: They are still unblooded, Catelyn thought as she watched Lord Bryce goad Ser Robar into juggling a brace of daggers.
asserted_relation: Bryce Caron was present at the feast at Bitterbridge on the night of the melee

evidence_quote: They are still unblooded, Catelyn thought as she watched Lord Bryce goad Ser Robar into juggling a brace of daggers.
asserted_relation: Robar Royce was present at the feast at Bitterbridge on the night of the melee

evidence_quote: Guyard the Green, who fancied himself a singer, diddled a harp and gave them a verse about tying lions' tails in knots
asserted_relation: Guyard Morrigen was present at Bitterbridge at the feast on the evening of the melee

evidence_quote: It was late afternoon when they sighted Riverrun, rising from the narrow point where the Tumblestone joined the Red Fork. The Tully castle looked like a great stone ship with its prow pointed downriver.
asserted_relation: The siege of Riverrun took place at Riverrun

evidence_quote: Ser Daven has command there. The Warden of the West. He has no need of me. Lancel does.
asserted_relation: Daven Lannister commanded the besieging forces at Riverrun as Warden of the West

evidence_quote: Your uncle is an old man. Valiant, yes, but the best part of his life is done. He has no bride to grieve for him, no children to defend. A good death is all the Blackfish can hope for . . . but you have years remaining, Edmure. And you are the rightful lord of House Tully, not him. Your uncle serves at your pleasure. The fate of Riverrun is in your hands.
asserted_relation: Jaime Lannister arrived to take overall command and negotiated the surrender of Riverrun

evidence_quote: Whilst I've been building rams and siege towers, Ryman Frey has raised a gibbet. Every day at dawn he brings forth Edmure Tully, drapes a noose around his neck, and threatens to hang him unless the castle yields.
asserted_relation: Ryman Frey commanded the Frey forces north of the Tumblestone during the siege

evidence_quote: The Blackfish expelled all the useless mouths from Riverrun and picked this country clean. He has enough stores to keep man and horse alive for two full years.
asserted_relation: Brynden Tully commanded the defense of Riverrun throughout the siege

evidence_quote: Ser Ryman Frey's encampment north of the Tumblestone was the largest, and the most disorderly. A great grey gallows loomed above the tents, as tall as any trebuchet. On it stood a solitary figure with a rope about his neck. Edmure Tully. Jaime felt a stab of pity. To keep him standing there day after day, with that noose around his neck . . . better to have his head off and be done with it.
asserted_relation: Edmure Tully was held hostage under a noose and coerced into surrendering Riverrun

evidence_quote: Lord Emmon Frey joined them, with his wife. Lady Genna claimed her stool with a look that dared any man there to question her presence. None did.
asserted_relation: Emmon Frey was present at the siege as the designated new Lord of Riverrun

evidence_quote: The Freys sent Ser Walder Rivers, called "Bastard Walder," and Ser Ryman's firstborn Edwyn, a pallid, slender man with a pinched nose and lank dark hair. Under a blue lambswool cloak, Edwyn wore a jerkin of finely tooled grey calfskin with ornate scrollwork worked into the leather. "I speak for House Frey," he announced.
asserted_relation: Edwyn Frey attended the siege as Frey representative and later inherited his father's command

evidence_quote: Two men did not choose to depart with the others. Ser Desmond Grell, Lord Hoster's old master-at-arms, preferred to take the black. So did Ser Robin Ryger, Riverrun's captain of guards. "This castle's been my home for forty years," said Grell.
asserted_relation: Desmond Grell was part of the Riverrun garrison during the siege

evidence_quote: Two men did not choose to depart with the others. Ser Desmond Grell, Lord Hoster's old master-at-arms, preferred to take the black. So did Ser Robin Ryger, Riverrun's captain of guards. "This castle's been my home for forty years," said Grell.
asserted_relation: Robin Ryger served as Riverrun's captain of guards during the siege

evidence_quote: Jeyne Westerling had been Robb Stark's queen, the girl who cost him everything. With a wolf in her belly, she could have proved more dangerous than the Blackfish.
asserted_relation: Jeyne Westerling was held inside Riverrun during the siege and released at surrender
